for_sathya_risks.headline: BRD Governance Breach + Oracle Scope Change [RISK]
for_sathya_risks.body: Ashwin Bhat Fugro shared the CM integration BRD directly with IT, bypassing the agreed channel (Abdul Ajees + Raj). Abdul Ajees was firm: "Look, we have clearly agreed before. Nothing has to be shared with anyone. It has to be shared with me and Sreshub." Abdul Ajees is preparing a formal email to enforce the process. Separately, the BRD scope must be revised — Oracle integration is now required for item master and vendor master in CM (previously excluded). (Owner: Ashwin Bhat Fugro)

for_sathya_risks.headline: Medicaid Integration Gap + PM Multi-Level Variance Limitation [RISK]
for_sathya_risks.body: Facilio is not sending the asset number back to the Medicaid system — Rebecca Barta flagged this: "Can you just make sure you're sending the asset number back over to Medicaid? They're not showing it." Additionally, the PM schedule inheritance logic does not support multi-level variance without manual duplication — a known functional gap. Michael DeRossett noted the workload: "you create extra work for myself Jesse. I don't have a lot of help anymore." Analysis and fix underway. (Owner: Jhimlee)

for_sathya_risks.headline: Open Dependencies: SLA Matrix + Security Deposit Data Pending [RISK]
for_sathya_risks.body: The move-in/move-out inspection process design has several open items: (1) SLA matrix by unit type (1BHK, 2BHK, etc.) not yet provided by Ahmad Turki/Saradi Mohamed, (2) FarVision must be configured to pass security deposit amount per tenant to Facilio to trigger the manual approval threshold, (3) updated process flow diagrams pending from Jhimlee by early next week. Risk of delay to go-live if these items are not closed promptly. (Owner: Jhimlee)

calls.title: Key Watcher Integration Testing
calls.summary: Working session between Facilio (Sathya, Sangavi, Ranjan, Vandhana, team) and CBRE for Key Watcher integration testing. The team was coordinating environment setup and contact with Key Watcher, with a Parallels activation prerequisite before enabling the Moboke integration. Minimal substantive dialogue captured in transcript — session was in early-stage testing coordination.
key_points: Integration testing session for Key Watcher system with CBRE team.
Team was coordinating contact with Key Watcher and resolving an environment issue ('paradise garden' environment noted as problematic).
Parallels activation required before enabling Moboke integration.
Mahesh (Facilio) was requested to join the session for support.
decisions: Parallels to be activated prior to enabling Moboke integration.
Session to continue with Magesh joining for technical support.

action_items.task: Contact Key Watcher and resolve paradise garden environment issue
Activate Parallels before proceeding with Moboke integration

calls.customer_sentiment: Neutral — call was a working session; no customer feedback captured in transcript.
calls.risks: Environment issue ('paradise garden') causing disruption to integration testing. Dependency on Key Watcher availability.
calls.open_dependencies: Key Watcher team availability; Parallels activation; Magesh joining the session.

calls.title: FarVision CM Integration Revisiting
calls.summary: Technical session to refine the CM integration architecture between Facilio, Oracle, and FarVision. Key outcome: Oracle will directly supply item master and vendor master data for CM (scope change from original BRD), with FarVision acting as a translator for PRs/POs. The call was disrupted by a governance issue — Ashwin had shared the BRD directly with IT, which Abdul Ajees explicitly rejected; a formal enforcement email is being prepared. The technical approach (GL codes + UDFs) was agreed and the BRD revision is due by Saturday.
key_points: Decision made: inventory will NOT be maintained in FarVision. Available spare parts to be uploaded to Facilio; Facilio integrates with Oracle for item master.
FarVision to act as a translator for PRs and POs — mapping text item references to Oracle item codes for GRN updates in Facilio.
Scope change: Oracle integration now required for CM item master and vendor master (previously scoped out). BRD must be revised.
BRD governance breach: Ashwin shared the BRD directly with IT, bypassing agreed channel. Abdul Ajees insisted all BRDs must go to him and Raj first.
Dheeraj proposed GL code approach: budget heads from FarVision created as GL codes in Facilio, with Oracle/FarVision references stored in UDF fields at GRN time.
decisions: Item master and vendor master for CM to integrate directly with Oracle (not FarVision). BRD scope updated accordingly.
No full item master sync in Facilio — GL code-level budget mapping adopted. Oracle/FarVision references captured in UDFs.
BRD distribution rule enforced: all future BRDs must be routed through Abdul Ajees and Raj only — not directly to IT.
Abdul Ajees to review the FarVision integration BRD by Saturday (9 May).

action_items.task: Revise BRD — remove 'no Oracle integration for CM' clause, add Oracle item master + vendor master scope
Share BRD with Dheeraj as requested
Issue formal email reinforcing BRD governance channel (all BRDs → Abdul Ajees + Raj only)

calls.customer_sentiment: Tense but functional — Abdul Ajees was visibly irritated by the BRD governance violation. Technical discussion was productive once refocused.
calls.escalations: Abdul Ajees enforced a governance escalation on BRD routing — was firm: "Let's follow one process, please." Preparing a formal email to all parties.
calls.date_shifts: BRD review deferred to Saturday 9 May.
calls.risks: Scope change on Oracle integration for CM increases integration complexity. BRD governance breach risks information control and stakeholder trust. FarVision item code mapping is complex — FarVision must maintain a reference table for GRN/stocking updates.
calls.open_dependencies: Ashwin BRD revision; Abdul Ajees formal governance email; FarVision item code mapping reference; Saturday BRD review.

calls.title: GECOMEP Implementation Progress Update
calls.summary: Very brief call to reschedule the GECOMEP implementation code review and progress status meeting to 3:00–4:00 PM Dubai time on the same day (8 May). No substantive implementation discussion took place. Customer team (Sakeena, Ahmad, Anto) was cooperative; internal scheduling constraints were the only friction noted.
key_points: Call was a brief scheduling exchange — the intended code review and progress status meeting was rescheduled to 3:00–4:00 PM Dubai time the same day.
Kesava noted a calendar conflict preventing attendance for the full slot; Arun Kumar available until 3:30 PM.
Jeeva assigned to send the revised meeting invitation.
decisions: Code review and implementation progress review rescheduled to 3:00–4:00 PM Dubai time (8 May).

action_items.task: Send revised meeting invitation for 3:00–4:00 PM Dubai time

calls.customer_sentiment: Neutral — brief, administrative call. Customer was cooperative and flexible.
calls.risks: Internal scheduling conflicts (Kesava and Arun Kumar availability gaps).
calls.open_dependencies: Jeeva to send meeting invite; Kesava and Arun Kumar to join within their availability windows.

calls.title: <MODON AM> : FarVision Move In-Move Out
calls.summary: Design session for the MODON move-in/move-out inspection process integrating Facilio and FarVision. Key outcomes: a security deposit-based threshold triggers manual PM approval for high-cost move-out damage, legal dispute scenarios put work orders in stagnant status with SLA clocks paused, and additional tenant-requested works during move-in follow a separate approval flow. Multiple open dependencies remain — SLA matrix by unit type, FarVision security deposit data integration, and portal feedback feature development — with updated diagrams due from Jhimlee early next week.
key_points: Move-out inspection: threshold mechanism agreed — if tenant-borne costs exceed the security deposit amount, property management manual approval is required before corrective work orders proceed.
Legal dispute scenario: work orders enter 'stagnant' status; SLA/KPI clock pauses until resolution.
Move-in inspection: all costs borne by property management. Additional tenant-requested works follow a separate flow with quotation, scope definition, and approval steps.
SLA/KPI matrix to be defined per unit type (1BHK, 2BHK, 3BHK, 4BHK) — Ahmad Turki and Saradi to provide this matrix.

Feedback mechanism: two channels confirmed — technician captures rating + signature in Facilio app (pushed to FarVision), and tenant submits detailed feedback via FarVision portal (three templates: move-in, move-out, maintenance).
FarVision portal feedback feature not yet built — to be developed.
PR for corrective work in move-out leasing vertical: Facilio → Oracle directly (FarVision handles AR only in leasing).
decisions: Cost threshold for manual PM approval = security deposit amount per tenant (FarVision to push this value to Facilio).
SLA clock pauses when tenant has not accepted the FMR report (move-out); move-in findings start work immediately.
Additional works during move-in are a separate flow from defect findings — separate FMR report, quotation, approval, and SLA rules.
Dual feedback system confirmed: Facilio app (technician-side) + FarVision portal (tenant-side).

action_items.task: Update process flow diagrams: add PM approval touchpoint for high-cost move-out scenarios, add additional works branch for move-in
Provide SLA matrix by unit type (1BHK, 2BHK, 3BHK, 4BHK → number of days)
Configure FarVision to push security deposit amount and unit type per tenant to Facilio
Build tenant feedback feature in FarVision portal (3 templates: move-in, move-out, maintenance)

calls.customer_sentiment: Engaged and constructive — Ahmad Turki actively shaped solution design, injecting new requirements around legal risk control and SLA nuance. No hostility.
calls.escalations: Ahmad Turki raised legal dispute scenario requiring work order stagnation and KPI hold — not an escalation against Facilio, but a product requirement driven by legal risk awareness.
calls.date_shifts: Updated process flow diagrams deferred to early next week. SLA matrix and FarVision data pending with no committed date.
calls.risks: Security deposit amount not yet in Facilio (FarVision dependency). SLA matrix undefined — go-live readiness at risk if not provided. FarVision portal feedback feature not yet built.
calls.open_dependencies: FarVision security deposit data push; Ahmad/Saradi SLA matrix; Jhimlee updated flow diagrams; FarVision portal feedback development.

calls.title: [THCE <> Facilio] : PM Discussion
calls.summary: Technical deep-dive on PM schedule configuration and parent-child asset relationships for THCE. Two key issues surfaced: (1) the PM multi-level variance architecture requires duplicating schedules at lower levels — a usability gap that creates risk of accidental overwrites and extra manual work; (2) Facilio is not returning the asset number to the Medicaid integration, a live bug flagged by Rebecca Barta. Jhimlee to analyse options for PM multi-level support and fix the Medicaid integration. Parent-child asset design for Da Vinci robots deferred to a follow-up session.
key_points: PM schedule configuration: assets can only have PMs at one of four schedule levels (site, model, asset category, individual). Overriding at a lower level requires duplicating the PM schedule, creating extra manual work.
Michael DeRossett flagged risk of accidental schedule overwrites: "if I push the wrong schedule I can mess up their pm schedules."
Michael noted bandwidth pressure: "I don't have a lot of help anymore."
Parent-child asset relationships: currently indicator-only — no automated workflows. Financial implications for contract pricing and cost allocation (Da Vinci robots).

Medicaid integration gap: Facilio not returning the asset number to the Medicaid system. Rebecca flagged: "Can you just make sure you're sending the asset number back over to Medicaid? They're not showing it."
Medicaid list view requirements unclear — Jhimlee to review separately with Rebecca.
No changes to be made until analysis is complete and documentation updated.
decisions: No PM schedule changes to be made pending Jhimlee's analysis on multi-level support options.
Parent-child asset model for Da Vinci robots to be fully scoped in a follow-up session (Jonathan Hill to be included).
Jhimlee to investigate and fix Medicaid asset number not being returned from Facilio.

action_items.task: Analyse PM schedule multi-level support — propose solutions (clone model-to-site option, or simultaneous multi-level support)
Investigate and fix Medicaid integration — ensure asset number is sent back to Medicaid system
Review Medicaid list view requirements separately with Rebecca Barta
Schedule parent-child asset follow-up session for Da Vinci robots (include Jonathan Hill)

calls.customer_sentiment: Mixed — constructive collaboration but undercurrent of frustration. Michael DeRossett is feeling bandwidth pressure and workaround burden. Rebecca Barta is pragmatic and assertive. No anger, but clear pressure signals.
calls.escalations: None formally. Jonathan Hill asserted he must be included in future parent-child discussions.
calls.date_shifts: Parent-child discussion and PM analysis decisions deferred pending analysis — no committed dates.
calls.risks: PM multi-level variance is a functional gap requiring workarounds and creating risk of accidental overwrites. Medicaid asset number integration is broken. Parent-child asset categories for Da Vinci robots have contract cost and regulatory tracking implications.
calls.open_dependencies: Jhimlee analysis on PM multi-level; Medicaid fix; Jonathan Hill availability for parent-child session; Rebecca follow-up on Medicaid list view.
